content.seo_title: For Students: SaaS Starter Kit to Scale AI Projects (Checklist)
content.seo_description: Overcome scaling hurdles with this SaaS starter kit checklist. Learn to use Antigravity and Claude Code to build professional-grade AI applications quickly.
content.answer_block: Stop reinventing the wheel. A SaaS starter kit provides the architectural scaffolding—auth, database, and payments—that beginners often trip over. By automating boilerplate code with Antigravity and Claude Code, you shift your focus from repetitive setup tasks to building high-value features that prove your technical competence to future employers.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a blank terminal, the weight of a thousand 'how-to' tutorials crushing your momentum. You want to build a real-world SaaS, but every time you start, you get lost in the labyrinth of database configuration and environment variables. The dream of a launched product is slowly suffocating under the complexity of manual setup.

But here is the truth: scaling isn't about working harder; it's about building smarter. By adopting a hardened starter kit, you stop playing 'system administrator' and start acting like a Lead Developer. We are going to leverage Antigravity for architectural scaffolding and Claude Code for rapid implementation, turning your bottleneck into a launchpad.

steps.title: Bootstrap the Architecture
steps.description: Initialize your project with a production-ready template to ensure you have a scalable foundation from day one.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a full-stack SaaS application with Next.js, Supabase, and Stripe integration. Include pre-configured authentication, user profile management, and a clean, responsive UI component library.

steps.title: Define Your Schema
steps.description: Map out your data relationships early to avoid refactoring headaches once your application starts growing.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a relational database schema for a multi-tenant SaaS application, including tables for users, subscription tiers, and project logs. Generate the Prisma schema file.

steps.title: Automate API Endpoints
steps.description: Stop writing boilerplate CRUD code. Use AI to generate robust API routes that follow industry standards.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create secure, authenticated API endpoints for the project resources. Implement rate limiting and comprehensive error handling for all POST and GET requests.

steps.title: Implement Stripe Payments
steps.description: Handle billing logic safely by integrating tested payment workflows into your starter kit.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure a Stripe webhook listener and subscription checkout flow. Ensure the database updates automatically when payment events are received.

steps.title: Deploy to Production
steps.description: Move your code from your local machine to the cloud, ensuring your environment variables are correctly mapped.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a deployment script for Vercel and optimize the build process for maximum performance and minimum cold-start times.

pro_tips: Never hardcode secrets; always use an .env file and ensure it is ignored by Git.
Prioritize 'Feature Flags' early so you can deploy incomplete features without breaking the production build.
Use TypeScript interfaces for your database models; it catches 90% of bugs before you even run the code.
Keep your database logic separate from your UI components to ensure your frontend stays lightweight and fast.
Always perform a 'security audit' prompt on your codebase every 500 lines to identify potential injection vulnerabilities.
content.next_steps: Once your base application is live, focus on building one unique 'killer feature' that differentiates your project from standard templates. Then, document your build process in a public GitHub repository to attract potential employers.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools, the blueprint, and the expertise to stop stalling and start shipping. Every day you wait is a day your portfolio stays empty. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
